level_1_criteria: No solution attempted. Cannot explain the concept or its purpose.
level_2_criteria: Partially correct solution with significant gaps. Misses best practices. No error handling.
level_3_criteria: Correct solution for common cases. Applies standard best practices. Some edge cases missed.
level_4_criteria: Correct, efficient, and well-structured. Handles edge cases. Explains trade-offs clearly.
level_5_criteria: Optimal solution with deep reasoning. Discusses architectural alternatives, complexity, and real-world constraints.
score_1_3_descriptor: Incorrect or no solution. Cannot explain reasoning.
score_4_6_descriptor: Partially correct. Some best practices. Misses edge cases.
score_7_10_descriptor: Correct, efficient, well-structured. Handles edge cases. Explains trade-offs.
auto_fail_triggers: Completely wrong answer on fundamental concept|Plagiarism detected|Cannot explain basic syntax|Blank response
bonus_indicators: Proposes novel optimization|References production battle-tested experience|Discusses complexity trade-offs|Open-source contribution mentioned
evaluator_notes: Calibrate against answer_calibration.csv for Technical domain.

level_1_criteria: Vague or no example given. Deflects responsibility. No structure.
level_2_criteria: Weak STAR structure. Generic example. Limited personal ownership or insight.
level_3_criteria: Clear STAR structure. Specific example. Adequate reflection on outcome.
level_4_criteria: Strong STAR with quantified impact. Clear ownership and lessons learned.
level_5_criteria: Exemplary STAR with measurable business impact, self-awareness, and systemic improvement demonstrated.
score_1_3_descriptor: Vague or generic. No specific examples. Deflects responsibility.
score_4_6_descriptor: Some structure. Moderate specificity. Limited reflection.
score_7_10_descriptor: Clear STAR format. Specific, quantified impact. Strong self-awareness and lessons learned.
auto_fail_triggers: No specific example provided after prompt|Blames others entirely without self-reflection|Fabricated scenario detected|Blank response
bonus_indicators: Quantified measurable impact|Systemic change resulted from experience|Clear growth demonstrated|Cross-functional leadership shown
evaluator_notes: Calibrate against answer_calibration.csv for Behavioral domain.

level_1_criteria: No coherent design. Cannot identify components or trade-offs.
level_2_criteria: Basic design with single-server thinking. Ignores scale, reliability, or maintainability.
level_3_criteria: Functional design addressing core requirements. Some scaling and fault tolerance considered.
level_4_criteria: Comprehensive design with scalability, reliability, and trade-off analysis. Well-communicated.
level_5_criteria: Production-grade design. Covers data flow, SLOs, failure modes, cost, and global distribution.
score_1_3_descriptor: No coherent design. Ignores scale. Cannot articulate trade-offs.
score_4_6_descriptor: Basic design. Considers some scaling factors. Limited trade-off discussion.
score_7_10_descriptor: Comprehensive design. Addresses all pillars. Clear trade-off analysis with justification.
auto_fail_triggers: Completely ignores scalability|No coherent design proposed|Fatal flaw in data model|Blank response
bonus_indicators: Addresses global distribution unprompted|Discusses cost optimization|Covers SLO/SLA proactively|Mentions chaos engineering
evaluator_notes: Calibrate against answer_calibration.csv for System Design domain.

level_1_criteria: No approach identified. Cannot solve even with hints.
level_2_criteria: Brute-force approach only. Correct output but O(n²) or worse. No complexity awareness.
level_3_criteria: Reasonable approach. Correct solution. Basic complexity analysis (O(n log n) or better).
level_4_criteria: Optimal approach with clear complexity analysis. Considers space/time trade-offs.
level_5_criteria: Multiple approaches compared. Optimal chosen with proof. Edge cases and constraints fully handled.
score_1_3_descriptor: No clear approach. Incorrect solution. Cannot analyze complexity.
score_4_6_descriptor: Reasonable approach. Brute force. Basic complexity awareness.
score_7_10_descriptor: Optimal approach. Correct. Clear O(n) analysis. Alternative solutions discussed.
auto_fail_triggers: Cannot decompose the problem|Random guessing with no reasoning|Completely wrong algorithmic approach|Blank response
bonus_indicators: Identifies multiple valid approaches and picks the best|Considers edge cases proactively|Discusses time and space complexity|Optimizes after initial solution
evaluator_notes: Calibrate against answer_calibration.csv for Problem Solving domain.

level_1_criteria: Superficial or incorrect knowledge. Cannot go beyond the definition.
level_2_criteria: Basic understanding of concepts. No awareness of current practices or real applications.
level_3_criteria: Solid foundational knowledge. Some practical application. Aware of recent developments.
level_4_criteria: Deep expertise with strong application examples. Up-to-date. Can compare approaches.
level_5_criteria: Expert-level breadth and depth. References primary sources. Demonstrates original thinking.
score_1_3_descriptor: Superficial knowledge. Outdated or inaccurate information.
score_4_6_descriptor: Solid fundamentals. Limited awareness of recent developments.
score_7_10_descriptor: Expert-level breadth and depth. Up-to-date. Strong practical application examples.
auto_fail_triggers: Fundamental domain misconception|Ignores core regulatory or compliance context|No industry awareness|Blank response
bonus_indicators: References specific regulation or standard|Competitive landscape awareness|Novel domain insight|Cross-domain application
evaluator_notes: Calibrate against answer_calibration.csv for Domain Knowledge domain.

level_1_criteria: Poor communication. Lacks empathy. Cannot adapt to feedback.
level_2_criteria: Basic communication. Shows some empathy but struggles with leadership or conflict.
level_3_criteria: Clear communication. Demonstrates empathy and basic leadership. Adapts well to feedback.
level_4_criteria: Strong communicator. Empathetic leader. Handles conflict professionally. Highly adaptable.
level_5_criteria: Exceptional communicator and leader. Inspires others. Masterful conflict resolution and adaptability.
score_7_10_descriptor: Strong communicator. Empathetic leader. Professional conflict resolution.
auto_fail_triggers: No specific example after prompting|Pure deflection of responsibility|Hostile or dismissive response|Blank response
bonus_indicators: Quantified business impact|Systemic improvement implemented|Cross-team influence demonstrated|Consistent pattern across examples

level_1_criteria: No clear vision or strategy. Struggles to build or lead a team.
level_2_criteria: Basic vision. Limited strategic thinking. Can lead a small team with guidance.
level_3_criteria: Clear vision and strategy. Successfully builds and leads teams. Mentors others.
level_4_criteria: Strong visionary and strategist. High-performing team builder. Proven execution and mentorship.
level_5_criteria: Transformational leader. Shapes industry-level strategy. Builds world-class teams. Master mentor.
score_7_10_descriptor: Strong vision. Proven team leadership and mentorship.
auto_fail_triggers: Claims zero leadership experience at senior level|Dismisses importance of team dynamics|No examples of decision-making|Blank response
bonus_indicators: Developed others who grew beyond initial scope|Built measurable team culture change|Led cross-org initiative|Successful turnaround story

level_1_criteria: Lacks statistical knowledge. Poor modeling choices. No data intuition.
level_2_criteria: Basic stats and modeling. Limited data intuition or visualization skills.
level_3_criteria: Solid statistical foundation. Appropriate modeling. Good data intuition and viz.
level_4_criteria: Advanced statistical rigor. Expert modeling. Strong data intuition and business impact.
level_5_criteria: Pioneering data science work. Novel modeling techniques. Exceptional business impact and viz.
score_7_10_descriptor: Advanced rigor. Expert modeling. Strong business impact.
auto_fail_triggers: Fundamental statistics error|Confuses correlation with causation|No mention of model validation|Blank response
bonus_indicators: Discusses model interpretability unprompted|Addresses data leakage|References A/B testing methodology|Considers model drift
